Elihu is also called Eliab and Eliel ; see 1 Chronicles 6:27 and 34.

An ephah is approximately 20 dry quarts or 22 liters (probably about 25.5 pounds or 11.6 kilograms of flour).

28.text: One DSS manuscript; MT he worshiped

1.text: Hebrew; LXX “Has not the LORD anointed you ruler over Israel? And you will rule over the LORD’s people and save them from their enemies around them. This will be the sign to you that the LORD has appointed you to be leader over His inheritance.

21.text: LXX includes And he brought the family of the Matrites near, man by man.

27.text: MT and LXX; One DSS manuscript includes Nahash, king of the Ammonites, had viciously oppressed the people of Gad and Reuben, gouging out the right eye of each Israelite dwelling there. He would not allow anyone to rescue them, and there was no Israelite east of the Jordan whose right eye had not been gouged out. But 7,000 men had escaped from the Ammonites and settled in Jabesh-gilead.

5.text: Hebrew Gibeath-Elohim , meaning the hill of God

1.text: DSS and LXX About a month later Nahash
Forms of the Hebrew berit are translated in most passages as covenant .

11.text: Jerubbaal is another name for Gideon and probably means let Baal contend ; see Judges 6:32.

14.text: then all will be well is implied; Literally If you fear the LORD and serve Him and obey His voice, and if you do not rebel against the command of the LORD, (then) both you and the king who reigns over you will follow the LORD your God.

1.text: A few late LXX manuscripts; MT Saul was a son of a year
Or over Israel forty years (see Acts 13:21); MT over Israel two years

15.text: LXX Then Samuel set out, and the rest of the people went up after Saul to meet the army; they went up from Gilgal to Gibeah of Benjamin.

20.text: LXX; Hebrew and plowshares ; (so plowshare appears twice in the Hebrew).

21.text: A pim possibly refers to a polished stone weighing approximately 0.25 ounces or 7 grams found in excavations. This is equivalent to about two-thirds of a shekel and likely refers to the price charged by the Philistines for the services listed.
Hebrew does not include the currency unit of a shekel charged for sharpening a pitchfork, an axe, or an oxgoad; alternatively, possibly a third of a pim for each.

5.text: Some LXX manuscripts and Syriac; Hebrew thirty thousand

14.text: Hebrew half a yoke . A yoke was the amount of land plowed by a pair of yoked oxen in one day.

15.text: Or and a terrible panic spread

16.text: Or melting away and going here and there

18.text: Hebrew; LXX “Bring the ephod.” For at that time he wore the ephod before the Israelites.

27.text: Or his strength was renewed ; similarly in verse 29

41.text: LXX and Vulgate; MT contains only the short quotation, “Give a perfect (lot).”

47.text: Or he inflicted punishment on them from Hebrew; LXX he was victorious

3.text: Forms of the Hebrew cherem refer to the giving over of things or persons to the LORD, either by destroying them or by giving them as an offering; also in verses 8, 9, 15, 18, 20, and 21.

Or “Surely this is the bitterness of death.” See DSS and LXX.

12.text: LXX and Syriac; Hebrew He had become advanced among men

17.text: An ephah is approximately 20 dry quarts or 22 liters of roasted grain.

4.text: Goliath was approximately 9 feet 9 inches or 297 centimeters tall; LXX, DSS, and Josephus four cubits and a span in height (approximately 6 feet 9 inches or 206 centimeters tall).

14.text: Forms of the Hebrew chesed are translated here and in most cases throughout the Scriptures as loving devotion ; the range of meaning includes love , goodness , kindness , faithfulness , and mercy , as well as loyalty to a covenant .
